channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials on Monday requested a court warrant to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3 amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu completes prostate surgery
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent a successful prostate removal surgery on Sunday, officials said. Netanyahu, 75, has had a series of health issues in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: South Korean families pray for relatives who vanished into North Korean prisons a decade ago
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In the past year, Choi Jin-young has done everything he can to bring attention to his father, a missionary who's serving a life sentence in North Korea. He’s met senior U.N., U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flights temporarily suspended at Halifax airport after airplane landing incident
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia (AP) — The Transportation Safety Board of Canada is investigating an incident involving a plane at the Halifax Stanfield International Airport, which one passenger described as a rough landing that sparked flames.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US investigators to assist South Korea with plane crash investigation
item.description: U.S. investigators are helping South Korea investigate the plane crash on Sunday that killed 179 people on board a plane from Thailand. The team of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South Korea plane's final moments captured on video before hitting concrete barrier, triggering explosion
item.description: A video released Sunday shows the exact moment a plane skidded off a runway in South Korea before crashing into a concrete barrier, causing an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kavelashvili is inaugurated as Georgia's president, complicating its path toward EU
item.description: TBILISI, Goergia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili was formally inaugurated as president of Georgia on Sunday, cementing the ruling party's grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's de facto leader says it could take up to 4 years to hold elections
item.description: Syria’s de facto leader has said it could take up to four years to hold elections in Syria, and that he plans on dissolving his Islamist group that led the country’s insurgency at an anticipated national dialogue summit for the country.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China directs largest military build-up since 1930s Nazi Germany, expert warns, citing Pentagon report
item.description: Defense Department report reveals China's rapidly growing weapons arsenal and "psychological warfare" operation ahead of new Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kim Jong Un vows to launch 'toughest' anti-US policy
item.description: Kim Jong Un vowed to launch a tough anti-U.S. strategy in the coming years, as President-elect Trump prepares to enter his second term in office. During a five-day Workers’ Party meeting this past week, Kim said North Korea would launch “the toughest anti-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Plane crash at South Korean airport kills all but 2 aboard
item.description: The death toll in a South Korean plane crash rose to 179, killing all but two people aboard, The New York Times reported, citing fire officials at the Muan International Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Plane skids off runway in South Korea, killing at least 177
item.description: A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eyewitnesses to South Korea plane crash recount sparks in engine, bird strike before collision
item.description: Witnesses to a deadly plane crash in South Korea said they observed flames in the engine of the aircraft, heard explosions and noticed birds colliding with the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli hospital says Netanyahu has undergone successful prostate surgery
item.description: An Israeli hospital says Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has undergone successful prostate surgery.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A fourth infant dies of the winter cold in Gaza as families share blankets in seaside tents
item.description: A fourth infant has died of hypothermia in Gaza, where hundreds of thousands of Palestinians displaced by nearly 15 months of war are huddled in tents along the rainy, windswept coast as winter arrives.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel detains the director of one of northern Gaza's last functioning hospitals during a raid
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel's army detained the director of one of northern Gaza 's last functioning hospitals as overnight strikes elsewhere in the territory killed nine people, including children, Palestinian medical officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House: Russia, North Korea treating troops as 'expendable' in conflict with Ukraine
item.description: White House National Security communications adviser John Kirby said Russia was treating North Korean troops as “expendable” by sending them on impractical ground operations in its war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Drought, fires and deforestation battered Amazon rainforest in 2024
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — 2024 was a brutal year for the Amazon rainforest, with rampant wildfires and extreme drought ravaging large parts of a biome that’s a critical counterweight to climate change. A warming climate fed drought that in turn fed the worst year for fires since 2005.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin apologizes over 'tragic' Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash
item.description: Russia’s President Vladimir Putin apologized to Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev over the “tragic” Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash that killed 38 people on board, but stopped short of admitting any responsibility for the incident.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: IDF finds Hezbollah weapons cache in underground tunnel: video
item.description: The Israeli military said it discovered and destroyed an underground tunnel system used by Hezbollah to hide weapons used to attack Israel in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leon Panetta suggests it's 'pretty clear' Russia 'responsible' for Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash
item.description: Former Secretary of Defense and CIA director Leon Panetta speculated that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says ‘early indications’ suggest Russian air defenses downed Azerbaijan flight
item.description: The White House said Friday initial signs pointed to Russian air defenses downing a passenger plane that crashed over Kazakhstan and killed 38 people. White House national security spokesperson John Kirby told reporters that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Azerbaijan Airlines blames deadly plane crash on 'external interference' as Russia speculation grows
item.description: Azerbaijan Airlines has suspended flights to multiple Russian airports in the wake of a deadly plane crash, which it attributed to "physical and technical external interference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about South Korean acting President Han's impeachment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The impeachment of South Korea's acting President Han Duck-soo Friday has plunged the country into further political turmoil, coming less than two weeks after lawmakers impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Armed for survival: How Oct 7 Hamas massacre transformed gun culture in Israel
item.description: In the wake of the unprecedented October. 7 Hamas terrorist attacks, Israelis are having to grapple with the new reality of firearm ownership in a country where owning guns was once uncommon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Croatia's outspoken president is seeking reelection. He faces several contenders in Sunday's vote
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s left-leaning president, an outspoken critic of Western military support for Ukraine in its war against Russia, is running for reelection this weekend against an array of contenders, including the candidate backed by the conservative government.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Aviation experts say Russia's air defense fire likely caused Azerbaijan plane crash as nation mourns
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday that Russian air defense fire was likely responsible for the Azerbaijani plane crash the day before that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
